name: Bern (Switzerland)
description: A square red field crossed by a gold diagonal bend from lower hoist to upper fly, charged with a black bear walking upward along the bend.
history: The bear is the canting arms of Bern (from German Baer); attested in the 13th century, the red field with a gold bend was fixed around 1289. The cantonal colours remain red and black.

features.symbolism: a gold bend on a red field

features.role: bear passant climbing the bend, armed and langued red
